assertion_results.text: The verification report marks all 5 acceptance criteria as PASS — the diff implements single license filter, comma-separated filter, 400 validation, pagination integration, and unchanged response shape
assertion_results.evidence: report.md summary table shows 'Acceptance Criteria | PASS'. The detailed Correctness section states 'All 5 acceptance criteria are satisfied by the PR diff.' Five separate criterion files confirm each: criterion-1.md (single license filter, PASS), criterion-2.md (multi-license filter, PASS), criterion-3.md (invalid license 400, PASS), criterion-4.md (pagination integration, PASS), criterion-5.md (response shape unchanged, PASS).

assertion_results.text: Scope containment is PASS — all files in the diff (list.rs, service/mod.rs, tests/api/package.rs) are listed in the task's Files to Modify or Files to Create
assertion_results.evidence: report.md 'Scope Containment: PASS' section lists Files to Modify (list.rs, service/mod.rs), Files to Create (tests/api/package.rs), and Files in PR diff (same three files), then states: 'The PR diff touches exactly the files specified in the task, with no extra files added or expected files missing. The set of modified and created files is a 1:1 match with the task specification.'

assertion_results.text: No sub-tasks are created because there are no review comments (Review Feedback is N/A)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md shows 'Review Feedback | N/A' in the summary table. The Review Feedback section states 'No review comments exist on this PR.' Root-Cause Investigation section states 'No sub-tasks were created from this task.' Convention Upgrade also confirms 'No review comments exist on this PR, so no suggestions to classify.'

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only report/analysis files: report.md and criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md. All content is analytical — code snippets appear only as quoted evidence within verification analysis. No code files appear in the outputs. The report reads and analyzes a PR diff without making any modifications.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR — it presents findings for human review (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md concludes with 'Overall: PASS' as a verdict but takes no merge action. There is no mention of merging, approving, or auto-merging the PR anywhere in any output file. The report presents a summary table of verdicts and detailed findings for each domain, structured for human review.

assertion_results.text: Sensitive pattern scan is PASS — the diff contains no passwords, API keys, or private keys
assertion_results.evidence: report.md Security section: 'Sensitive Pattern Scan: PASS'. Lists scanned patterns: 'Hardcoded passwords or secrets, API keys or tokens, Private keys or certificates, Connection strings with credentials, Environment variable references to secrets.' States: 'No sensitive patterns were detected. The diff contains only Rust source code for query parameter parsing, SPDX license validation, database query filtering, and test assertions. No credentials, keys, or secrets are present in any added line.'

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/package.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md summary table: 'Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE'. The detailed section states: 'The file `tests/api/package.rs` is a new file (`new file mode 100644` in the diff header). No existing test files were modified or had tests removed. All test changes are purely additive.'

assertion_results.text: The verification report assembles verdicts from all four domain sub-agents: Scope Containment, Diff Size, and Commit Traceability from Intent Alignment; Sensitive Patterns from Security; CI Status, Acceptance Criteria, and Verification Commands from Correctness; Test Quality (combining Repetitive Test Detection, Test Documentation, and Eval Quality) and Test Change Classification from Style/Conventions
assertion_results.evidence: report.md is organized into four domain sections: (1) '## Intent Alignment' containing Scope Containment (PASS), Diff Size (PASS), Commit Traceability (PASS); (2) '## Security' containing Sensitive Pattern Scan (PASS); (3) '## Correctness' containing CI Status (PASS), Acceptance Criteria (PASS), Verification Commands (N/A); (4) '## Style/Conventions' containing Test Quality (PASS) — which explicitly combines 'Repetitive Test Detection: PASS', 'Test Documentation: PASS', and 'Eval Quality: N/A' — and Test Change Classification (ADDITIVE). All four domains and all specified sub-verdicts are present.

assertion_results.text: The report includes detailed findings with specific evidence for each domain — file-by-file scope comparison (Intent Alignment), line-level pattern scanning results (Security), per-criterion code-level verification (Correctness), and test quality assessment (Style/Conventions) — not just pass/fail verdicts in the summary table
assertion_results.evidence: Intent Alignment: file-by-file comparison listing 'Files to Modify' (list.rs, service/mod.rs), 'Files to Create' (tests/api/package.rs), and 'Files in PR diff' with per-file status (modified/new). Security: enumerates specific pattern categories scanned ('Hardcoded passwords or secrets, API keys or tokens, Private keys or certificates...') and describes diff content ('Rust source code for query parameter parsing...'). Correctness: five separate criterion files (criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md) each contain code-level evidence with Rust code snippets from the diff showing exact implementation details. Style/Conventions: per-test analysis listing all 4 test functions with descriptions, doc comment verification with exact comment text quoted, and test change classification with diff header evidence.

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR — no reviews match the eval result detection criteria, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence: report.md Test Quality section states 'Eval Quality: N/A' with explanation 'No eval result reviews exist for this PR.' The combination logic is explicit: 'Since all non-N/A sub-checks are PASS, Test Quality = PASS.' This confirms Eval Quality (N/A) does not affect the combination — only Repetitive Test Detection (PASS) and Test Documentation (PASS) determine the result.

assertion_results.text: No eval failure sub-tasks are created because Eval Quality is N/A — eval failure sub-tasks are only created when Eval Quality is WARN (at least one eval assertion failed)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md shows Eval Quality as N/A ('No eval result reviews exist for this PR.') and Root-Cause Investigation as N/A ('No sub-tasks were created from this task.'). No mention of eval failure sub-tasks appears anywhere in the outputs, consistent with no sub-tasks being created when Eval Quality is N/A rather than WARN.

assertion_results.text: Review Feedback and Root-Cause Investigation verdicts are determined by the orchestrator independently from domain analysis — Review Feedback is N/A because no review comments exist, Root-Cause Investigation is N/A because no sub-tasks were created
assertion_results.evidence: report.md places Review Feedback and Root-Cause Investigation as separate top-level sections outside the four domain sections (Intent Alignment, Security, Correctness, Style/Conventions). Review Feedback appears at line 145: 'Review Feedback: N/A — No review comments exist on this PR.' Root-Cause Investigation at line 149: 'Root-Cause Investigation: N/A — No sub-tasks were created from this task.' Both appear in the summary table as independent rows alongside domain verdicts, and their reasoning is self-contained (no review comments / no sub-tasks), not derived from domain analysis.
